searchDocs.title: Setting SoundGroups​
searchDocs.content: SoundGroups Are Automatically determined in the Sounds Folder, There is no need to manually set it yourself.  The First Set of children in the Sound folder must be Configs/Folders as the name of that folder is used to determine the sound group. And Sounds under the sub-folder is automatically grouped.

searchDocs.title: Wally Configuration​
searchDocs.content: Once Wally is installed, run wally init on your project directory, and then add VluxySF as a dependency. For example, the following could be a wally.toml file for a project that includes a few of these modules:  [package] name = "your_name/your_project" version = "0.1.0" registry = "https://github.com/UpliftGames/wally-index" realm = "shared" [dependencies] VluxySF = "greenviper126/VluxySF@^1"   To install this dependency, run wally install within your project. Wally will create a Package folder in your directory with the installed dependency.

searchDocs.title: Rojo Configuration​
searchDocs.content: The Package folder created by Wally should be synced into Roblox Studio through your Rojo configuration. For instance, a Rojo configuration might have the following entry to sync the Packages folder into ReplicatedStorage:  { "name": "vluxysf", "tree": { "$className": "DataModel", "ReplicatedStorage": { "$className": "ReplicatedStorage", "Packages": { "$path": "Packages" } } } }

searchDocs.title: Setup​
searchDocs.content: In order to start using VluxySF you need to initialize the package on both the server and client.

searchDocs.title: SoundFolder​
searchDocs.content: In order to setup the soundFolder see this page Configuring Sounds

searchDocs.title: Server​
searchDocs.content: On the server you are required to inject the config/folder location of all your instanced sounds. This Folder should be located on the server.  local ServerStorage = game:GetService("ServerStorage") local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) --folder/config of the actual sounds you want to use. local soundFolder = ServerStorage:FindFirstChild("SOUNDS") --initialize the server VluxySF._initServer(soundFolder)

searchDocs.title: Client​
searchDocs.content: local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) --initialize the client VluxySF._initClient()

searchDocs.title: SoundEffectDefinition​
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundEffectDefinition = Dictionary<any> Defines the properties of a sound effect as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: SoundEffectDefinitions​
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundEffectDefinitions = Dictionary<SoundEffectDefinition> Defines the sound effects of a sound as a dictionary of class names to their corresponding properties.

searchDocs.title: SoundProperties​
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundProperties = Dictionary<any> Defines the properties of a sound as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: SoundDefinition​
searchDocs.content: </> interface SoundDefinition { Properties: SoundProperties-- The properties of the sound itself, such as SoundId, Volume, etc. Effects: SoundEffectDefinitions-- The sound effects of the sound, organized by class name. } Defines the properties and sound effects of a sound.

searchDocs.title: SoundDefinitions​
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundDefinitions = Dictionary<SoundDefinition> Defines the sound definitions of the system as a dictionary of sound names to their corresponding sound data.

searchDocs.title: SoundGroups​
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundGroups = Dictionary<SoundGroup > Defines the type of a sound group instance.

searchDocs.title: SoundSchema​
searchDocs.content: </> interface SoundSchema { UnattachedSounds: Configuration -- A folder for storing unattached sounds. SoundGroupContainer: Configuration -- A folder for storing sound groups. SoundGroups: SoundGroups-- A dictionary of sound group instances, organized by name. SoundDefinitions: SoundDefinitions-- A dictionary of sound definitions, organized by sound name. PreloadIds: {string}-- A list of sound asset IDs to preload. } Defines the overall schema for the sound system, containing all necessary data for sound creation and management.

searchDocs.title: GetSoundDefinitions​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.GetSoundDefinitions() → SoundDefinitions-- All converted sound data Returns all serialized sound data.

searchDocs.title: GetSoundLocations​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.GetSoundLocations() → {string}-- All registered sound locations Returns all sound locations.

searchDocs.title: GetSoundGroup​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.GetSoundGroup( name: string-- Name of the sound group ) → SoundGroup? -- Matching sound group, if found Finds a sound group by name.

searchDocs.title: GetSoundGroups​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.GetSoundGroups() → SoundGroups-- All sound groups Returns all sound groups.

searchDocs.title: GetSoundGroupNames​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.GetSoundGroupNames() → {string}-- All registered sound group names Returns all sound group names.

searchDocs.title: Create​
searchDocs.content: SoundEntity </> VluxySF.Create( sound: string | SoundDefinition-- The sound location or a sound definition to attach to. ) → SoundEntity-- Constructed class A constructor for manipulating your sounds.

searchDocs.title: ConstructSoundFromDefinition​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.ConstructSoundFromDefinition( soundDefinition: SoundDefinition-- The sound definition to construct from. ) → Sound -- Constructed sound instance based on the provided definition. Constructs a sound instance from a given sound definition, applying all specified properties and effects.

searchDocs.title: CreateSoundDefinitionFromSound​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.CreateSoundDefinitionFromSound( sound: Sound -- The sound instance to convert into a sound definition. ) → SoundDefinition-- A sound definition representing the provided sound instance. Creates a sound definition from a Roblox Sound instance by serializing its properties and valid child effects. This does not get added to the definitions list.

searchDocs.title: ConstructSoundFromName​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> VluxySF.ConstructSoundFromName( name: string-- The name of the sound definition to construct from. ) → Sound? -- Constructed sound instance if the definition is found, otherwise nil. Constructs a sound instance from a sound definition identified by its name.

searchDocs.title: GetEstimatedPlayLength​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.GetEstimatedPlayLength( sound: Sound -- The sound instance to analyze. ) → number-- The estimated play length of the sound, including any variations in playback speed. Calculates the estimated play length of a sound instance by considering its SoundId and any attached sound effects that may influence its duration.

searchDocs.title: Variance​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.Variance( min: number?,-- Minimum multiplier for variance (default: 0.95) max: number?-- Maximum multiplier for variance (default: 1.05) ) → () → number-- variance result Calculates a random variance value within the specified range.

searchDocs.title: PreloadCallback​
searchDocs.content: </> type PreloadCallback = ( assetId: string, assetFetchStatus: Enum.AssetFetchStatus ) → () Callback function that PreLoadAsync uses.

searchDocs.title: EventCallback​
searchDocs.content: </> type EventCallback = ( v1: any, v2: any ) → () Generic callback function for events. The parameters passed to the callback will depend on the specific event being listened to.

searchDocs.title: InstanceEventTypes​
searchDocs.content: </> type InstanceEventTypes = "Changed" | "AncestryChanged" | "AttributeChanged" | "ChildAdded" | "ChildRemoved" | "DescendantAdded" | "DescendantRemoving" | "Destroying" | "StyledPropertiesChanged" List of instance event types.

searchDocs.title: SoundEventTypes​
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundEventTypes = "DidLoop" | "Ended" | "Loaded" | "Paused" | "Played" | "Resumed" | "Stopped" List of sound event types.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity.new( sound: string | SoundDefinition-- The name of the sound definition to use or a full sound definition table. ) → SoundEntity-- A new instance of SoundEntity initialized with the specified sound. Constructor

searchDocs.title: GetRawSound​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:GetRawSound() → Sound -- The raw Sound instance created by this SoundEntity. Provides access to the underlying Sound instance for advanced manipulation or retrieval of properties.

searchDocs.title: ResetToDefinition​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:ResetToDefinition() → SoundEntity Resets the sound instance to match the original sound definition.

searchDocs.title: Fork​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:Fork( thread: function(self: SoundEntity)-- The function to run in a new thread, which receives the SoundEntity instance as an argument. ) → SoundEntity Starts a new thread to run the provided function, allowing you to perform concurrent operations on the sound while it is playing.

searchDocs.title: Preload​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> SoundEntity:Preload( callback: PreloadCallback?-- Optional callback to handle the sound successfully preloading or failing to preload. ) → SoundEntity Preloads the sound's audio asset to reduce latency when it is first played. This applies to all future sounds with the same assetId. Doing this increases memory usage on the client and is not reversible. Only use if needed!

searchDocs.title: Sleep​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> SoundEntity:Sleep( time: number-- The amount of time in seconds to wait. ) → SoundEntity Waits for the specified amount of time before resuming. This can be used to create delays in sound sequences or effects.

searchDocs.title: RunCustom​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> SoundEntity:RunCustom( func: function(self: SoundEntity)-- The custom function to run. ) → () Runs a custom function of your choice if code needs to run in a certain sequence.

searchDocs.title: Await​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> SoundEntity:Await( timeout: number?-- Optional timeout in seconds to wait before automatically cleaning up the sound. If not provided, it will wait indefinitely until the sound finishes playing. ) → () Waits for the sound to finish playing or for the specified timeout to elapse before cleaning up the sound instance.

searchDocs.title: PlayWhenLoaded​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> SoundEntity:PlayWhenLoaded() → SoundEntity Plays the sound when it is fully loaded.

searchDocs.title: PlayOnce​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:PlayOnce() → () Plays the sound and automatically cleans it up after it finishes. This is useful for one-off sound effects that don't need to be reused.

searchDocs.title: Reset​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:Reset() → SoundEntity Stops and Replays the sound from the beginning. This is useful for quickly restarting a sound without having to create a new SoundEntity instance.

searchDocs.title: Clone​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:Clone() → SoundEntity-- A new SoundEntity instance with the same properties and effects as the original. Creates a clone of the SoundEntity, allowing you to create multiple instances of the same sound definition without modifying the original.

searchDocs.title: Extend​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:Extend() → SoundEntity-- A new SoundEntity instance with the same properties and effects as the original. Makes a clone that is connected to the original SoundEntity. If the original is cleaned up so is this instance.

searchDocs.title: Cleanup​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:Cleanup() → () Cleans up the Instance.

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:Destroy() → () Alias for Cleanup. Cleans up the Instance.

searchDocs.title: FadeToRaw​
searchDocs.content: VolumeFade </> SoundEntity:FadeToRaw( volume: number,-- The target volume to fade to. tweenInfo: TweenInfo -- The TweenInfo object defining the duration and easing style of the fade. ) → SoundEntity Fades the sound's volume to the specified level over time using a tween.

searchDocs.title: FadeTo​
searchDocs.content: VolumeFade </> SoundEntity:FadeTo( volume: number,-- The target volume to fade to. time: number?,-- The duration of the fade in seconds. Defaults to 1 second if not provided. style: Enum.EasingStyle?,-- Optional easing style for the fade. Defaults to Sine if not provided. direction: Enum.EasingDirection?-- Optional easing direction for the fade. Defaults to Out if not provided. ) → SoundEntity Similar to FadeToRaw but does not need tweenInfo to be passed in.

searchDocs.title: FadeIn​
searchDocs.content: VolumeFade </> SoundEntity:FadeIn( time: number,-- The duration of the fade in seconds. style: Enum.EasingStyle?-- Optional easing style for the fade. Defaults to Sine. ) → SoundEntity Fades the sound's volume up from 0 to its defined volume over the specified duration using a tween.

searchDocs.title: FadeOut​
searchDocs.content: VolumeFade </> SoundEntity:FadeOut( time: number,-- The duration of the fade out in seconds. style: Enum.EasingStyle?-- Optional easing style for the fade out. Defaults to Sine. ) → SoundEntity Fades the sound's volume down to 0 over the specified duration using a tween.

searchDocs.title: FadeStop​
searchDocs.content: VolumeFade </> SoundEntity:FadeStop() → SoundEntity Stops any active volume tween and retains the current volume level.

searchDocs.title: Play​
searchDocs.content: SoundMethod </> SoundEntity:Play() → SoundEntity Plays the sound.

searchDocs.title: Pause​
searchDocs.content: SoundMethod </> SoundEntity:Pause() → SoundEntity Pauses the sound.

searchDocs.title: Resume​
searchDocs.content: SoundMethod </> SoundEntity:Resume() → SoundEntity Resumes the sound if it was paused.

searchDocs.title: Stop​
searchDocs.content: SoundMethod </> SoundEntity:Stop() → SoundEntity Stops the sound.

searchDocs.title: Listen​
searchDocs.content: SoundEvent </> SoundEntity:Listen( eventType: SoundEventTypes,-- The type of sound event to listen to. func: EventCallback-- The callback function to execute when the event occurs. ) → Trove  Listens to a specific event on the Sound instance and executes the provided callback function when the event occurs.

searchDocs.title: SetParent​
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetParent( parent: Instance -- The new parent for the sound. ) → SoundEntity Sets the parent property of the underlying Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: SetLooped​
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetLooped( looped: boolean-- Whether the sound should loop or not. ) → SoundEntity Sets the Looped property of the underlying Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: SetArchivable​
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetArchivable( archivable: boolean-- Whether the sound should be archivable. ) → SoundEntity Sets the Archivable property of the underlying Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: SetPlayOnRemove​
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetPlayOnRemove( playOnRemove: boolean-- Whether the sound should play when removed. ) → SoundEntity Sets the PlayOnRemove property of the underlying Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: SetLoopRegion​
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetLoopRegion( loopRegion: NumberRange -- The loop region range in seconds. ) → SoundEntity Sets the LoopRegion property of the underlying Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: SetPlaybackRegion​
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetPlaybackRegion( region: NumberRange -- The playback region range in seconds. ) → SoundEntity Sets the PlaybackRegion property of the underlying Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: SetPlaybackRegionsEnabled​
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetPlaybackRegionsEnabled( enabled: boolean-- Whether playback regions should be enabled. ) → SoundEntity Sets the PlaybackRegionsEnabled property of the underlying Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: SetPlaybackSpeed​
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetPlaybackSpeed( speed: number-- The playback speed multiplier. ) → SoundEntity Sets the PlaybackSpeed property of the underlying Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: SetRollOffMaxDistance​
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetRollOffMaxDistance( maxDistance: number-- The maximum distance in studs. ) → SoundEntity Sets the RollOffMaxDistance property of the underlying Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: SetRollOffMinDistance​
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetRollOffMinDistance( minDistance: number-- The minimum distance in studs. ) → SoundEntity Sets the RollOffMinDistance property of the underlying Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: SetRollOffMode​
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetRollOffMode( rollOffMode: Enum.RollOffMode-- The roll off mode to use. ) → SoundEntity Sets the RollOffMode property of the underlying Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: SetSoundGroup​
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetSoundGroup( soundGroup: SoundGroup -- The sound group to assign. ) → SoundEntity Sets the SoundGroup property of the underlying Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: SetTimePosition​
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetTimePosition( timePosition: number-- The time position in seconds. ) → SoundEntity Sets the TimePosition property of the underlying Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: SetVolume​
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetVolume( volume: number-- The volume level (0-10). ) → SoundEntity Sets the Volume property of the underlying Sound instance.
